stories.title: Washington Post's David Ignatius says war on Iran won't change the country's regime
stories.description: Michel Martin speaks to Washington Post columnist David Ignatius, who writes that the U.S. war on Iran, despite any tactical success, leaves the regime in place for the foreseeable future.

stories.title: Trump threatens NATO allies over Strait of Hormuz help
stories.description: With the Iran war in its third week and the price of oil reaching nearly $105 a barrel on Monday, President Trump again urged NATO and China to help secure the vital Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Iran blocks vital oil route as Israel expands its ground operations in Lebanon
stories.description: As the war in Iran enters its third week, there's no end in sight. Iran has blocked the Strait of Hormuz, a vital oil route, as Trump urges allies to act and Israel expands its ground operations.

stories.title: Iran expert on what the country's leaders think about the war with the US and Israel
stories.description: NPR's Michel Martin asks Iran expert Karim Sadjadpour how leaders in Tehran are thinking about the war with the U.S. and Israel.

stories.title: 5th member of Iranian women's soccer team gives up asylum in Australia
stories.description: The player's departure shortly before midnight on Sunday leaves two of an initial seven squad members in Australia.

stories.title: An Iranian-American writer describes the "vertigo" he feels about the war
stories.description: Host Adrian Ma speaks with Iranian American writer Nick Mafi about the war in Iran. Mafi says many Iranians in the US are feeling a sense of vertigo because of the conflict.

stories.title: Iran hits key UAE oil port and Dubai airport
stories.description: The port of Fujairah plays a crucial role in helping keep global supplies moving when the Strait of Hormuz is blocked.

stories.title: Who wants what from the Iran war?
stories.description: Most people, although not everybody, want this war to end as quickly as possible. But on what terms?

stories.title: Iran taking steps to prevent anti-establishment protests, Tehran residents tell BBC
stories.description: Residents describe new security checkpoints around the capital, where they say people are stopped and searched.

stories.title: What Iranians are being told about the war
stories.description: Iranian state media's coverage of the war includes inflations of enemy casualties and digital manipulation intended to glorify Iran.

stories.title: Iran captain latest footballer to drop asylum bid, Iranian state media says
stories.description: Five of the seven members of the women's football delegation who originally sought to stay in Australia have now chosen to return home.

stories.title: US names six crew killed in refuelling plane crash in Iraq
stories.description: The aircraft was on a combat mission as part of ongoing US operations against Iran when it crashed in western Iraq on Thursday.

stories.title: BBC visits aftermath of Israeli strike on Lebanon that killed family as IDF targets Hezbollah
stories.description: A relative tells BBC those killed were civilians and not Hezbollah operatives, but the Israel Defense Forces says it was targeting "terrorist infrastructure".

stories.title: Why has the US targeted Iran's Kharg Island?
stories.description: The tiny island is home to one of the most critical pieces of Iran's energy infrastructure.

stories.title: Hamas urges key ally Iran to halt attacks on Gulf states
stories.description: The Palestinian group also affirms Tehran's right to defend itself from "aggression" by the US and Israel.

stories.title: Bodies of 84 Iranian sailors killed in US torpedo strike to be repatriated
stories.description: The seamen died when Iranian warship the Iris Dena was sunk on 4 March by a US submarine near Sri Lanka.

stories.title: Emirati minister tells BBC Iran must end strikes on Gulf
stories.description: The UAE says more than 1,800 drones and missiles have been projected at the country since the war began.

stories.title: 'Fingers on the trigger': Deadly warnings for Iranians being urged to take action
stories.description: Iran's police chief has warned that his forces would treat anyone taking to the streets "at the enemy's request" as an enemy.

stories.title: Why did US and Israel attack Iran and how long could the war last?
stories.description: Conflict has spread across the Middle East since the US and Israel launched wide-ranging strikes on Iran on 28 February.

stories.title: How Iranians are evading internet blocks to contact family abroad
stories.description: Iranians are finding tech-savvy ways to get through the regime's restrictions on phone and internet connections.

stories.title: Under drone fire, exiled Kurds wait to confront Iranian regime
stories.description: A group of fighters based in Iraq say they are "ready to go home", as they face drone attacks from Tehran.

stories.title: Trump presses China, says NATO faces 'very bad' future if allies don't help open Hormuz Strait
stories.description: US President Donald Trump on Sunday sought to pressure NATO allies and China to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz, the critical oil transport conduit that Iran has effectively closed in retaliation for the US and Israeli war against Tehran. Global oil prices have surged by 40 to 5...

stories.title: Israel expands its ground operation in southern Lebanon
stories.description: The Israeli army says it has begun limited targeted ground operations against Hezbollah in South Lebanon. It comes three weeks after the US and Israel began carrying out strikes in Iran, a war that has since escalated throughout the Middle East, and beyond. Since the start of the...

stories.title: UK won't commit soldiers to a war without 'legal basis' amid Trump threats
stories.description: US President Donald Trump has warned allies to help secure the Strait of Hormuz, and that refusal would mean NATO would face a 'very bad' future. Addressing the war, the British Prime Minister point blank rejected the threat, saying the UK would not commit to a war unless it had ...

stories.title: Answering Trump's call for help in Middle East 'political suicide' for allies
stories.description: President Donald Trump urged NATO partners and China to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz as it remains closed due to the ongoing war in the Middle East triggered by the US-Israeli military campaign against Iran. FRANCE 24's Oliver Farry looks at why Trump's plan could be easier s...

stories.title: IDF launches 'limited operation' in Lebanon as 'buffer zone expands significantly'
stories.description: The Israeli military said on March 16 it had begun what it described as "limited ground operations" against Hezbollah in southern Lebanon.The announcement echoes similar statements issued in 2024, when Israel and Hezbollah fought a major war in Lebanon, and in 2023, when the mili...

stories.title: Israel pounds Beirut's southern suburbs with death toll raising to 850
stories.description: An Israeli strike hit Beirut's southern suburbs on March 15 according to Lebanese media. Israel's military issued an evacuation warning in the morning covering several neighbourhoods in the area. Lebanese authorities said the death toll in Israeli attacks rose to 850, while more ...

stories.title: Trump calls for help on securing Strait of Hormuz as oil prices surge
stories.description: US President Donald Trump is turning to other countries for help policing the Strait of Hormuz, effectively closed by Iran for the US and its allies after the US and Israel launched the war in Tehran. Since the closing of the crucial passageway, through which one fifth the world'...

stories.title: Trump announces AI chatbot ban while Pentagon uses it to prepare Iran attacks
stories.description: In the hours leading up to the US attack on Iran, Donald Trump announced that technology company Anthropic would be banned from all work with the federal government. At the same time, Anthropic's artificial intelligence chatbot Claude was reportedly being used by the Pentagon to ...

stories.title: Gulf States still under Iranian fire as Trump boasts of successful US strikes
stories.description: On the 17th day of the war, a ceasfire looks like a far off hope. While Donald Trump boasts of the success of the American strikes, which are supposed to have virtually destroyed Iranian military capabilities, the air defenses of the Gulf states—allies of the United States are op...

stories.title: India faces dilemma as energy crisis bites amid US trade deal negotiations
stories.description: New Delhi walks a policy tightrope, with public anger mounting over shortages of cooking gas as the US demands support for its war on Iran. Also in this edition: China's economy turns in stronger than expected figures, and oil prices continue to rise as Washington threatens to st...

stories.title: US says it has destroyed Iran missile capacity: How is Iran still shooting?
stories.description: Despite degraded launch capabilities, Iran has enough missiles to fire strategically, keep region on edge, say experts.

stories.title: Qatar calls on Iran to stop Gulf attacks, urges diplomatic solution
stories.description: Qatar foreign ministry spokesman Majed al-Ansari accuses Iran of targeting civilian areas as he calls for de-escalation.

stories.title: The US-Israeli strategy against Iran is working. Here is why
stories.description: Every aspect of Iran’s ability to project regional power is being successfully degraded.

stories.title: Qatar insists Iran is targeting civilian areas
stories.description: Qatar’s Foreign Ministry spokesperson Majed Al Ansari says Iranian attacks have targeted residential areas.

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz: Which countries’ ships has Iran allowed safe passage to?
stories.description: Tehran allows a small number of ships from certain countries to pass through waterway.

stories.title: South Korean protesters criticise Trump over Hormuz security demand
stories.description: Activists in South Korea have staged a protest against Donald Trump’s request for help securing the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Iran still playing in World Cup 2026 despite withdrawal rumours, AFC says
stories.description: Asian Football Confederation says it hasn't received any notification of Iran pulling out of upcoming World Cup.

stories.title: No, MAGA is not divided on the Iran war
stories.description: Trump’s supporters remain loyal, even if there are a few loud dissenting voices.

stories.title: War on Iran: Can fuel rationing, remote work, short sleeves ease oil woes?
stories.description: Rising oil prices amid the US-Israel war on Iran are pushing nations to readjust their energy policies.

stories.title: How Israel, the US and Iran deploy covert ops
stories.description: Sometimes called sleeper cells, these anonymous agents employ deception to spy, sabotage or kill.

stories.title: Map shows how 16 days of attacks have evolved in US-Israel war on Iran
stories.description: Here's a day-by-day breakdown of attacks, illustrating the targets hit and the weapons used since February 28.

stories.title: Hamas holds talks with Trump-led board as Iran war strains Gaza plan: sources
stories.description: The envoys from US President Donald Trump’s “Board of Peace” have met representatives of Hamas in Cairo in an effort to safeguard the Gaza ceasefire, under serious strain since the United States and Israel began bombing Iran, three sources told Reuters. The weekend meeting is the...

stories.title: Tehran thanks Islamabad for extending ‘strong solidarity’ with Iranian govt, people facing US-Israeli aggression
stories.description: Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi on Monday thanked the government and people of Pakistan for extending their “strong solidarity and support” with Iran and its people who he said were facing aggression from the United States and Israel. In a post in Urdu on X, Araghchi said:...

stories.title: US wants NATO, Arab nations to do more to open up Strait of Hormuz — White House
stories.description: White House Spokesperson Karoline Leavitt stressed that it was NATO that needed to step up the most

stories.title: Kiev's unwillingness to negotiate, Iran war fallout: Lavrov's statements
stories.description: According to the Russian top diplomat, the EU has "fully" discredited itself and there is nothing constructive in its position for progress in the negotiations on Ukraine

stories.title: Iran may draw US into war of attrition — Tunisian military expert
stories.description: According to Tunisian military expert Abdel Hamid Mahfouzi, while the United States and Israel maintain a clear technological edge, Iran possesses a range of strategic advantages that complicate the regional security landscape

stories.title: Zelensky drags Ukraine into conflict with Iran — Iranian diplomat
stories.description: This only means complicity in the conflict and cooperation with the aggressors against Iran, which will entail international responsibility for this state, and the Iranians will never forgive this, Esmail Baghaei stated

stories.title: Iran continues cooperating with Russia despite conflict — diplomat
stories.description: All the agreements and treaties between Iran and Russia as part of the strategic cooperation and partner relations are still in force, Iranian foreign ministry spokesman Esmail Baghaei stressed

stories.title: US allies at a loss as to Washington's endgame in conflict with Iran — Bloomberg
stories.description: According to the agency’s information, G7 leaders sought clarification from Donald Trump during a recent conference call

stories.title: US, Israel may use copycat Iranian arms for false-flag attacks in region — Iranian MFA
stories.description: Spokesman Esmail Baghaei added that some countries of the region had been in strained relations with Iran for a long time

stories.title: US allows Iranian tankers to cross Strait of Hormuz — Treasury secretary
stories.description: According to Scott Bessent, some Chinese ships are also leaving the Persian Gulf

stories.title: Hackers erase 12 petabytes of data in cyberattack on US medical company — Iranian TV
stories.description: The hackers warned that it was "just the beginning"

stories.title: US can not stop its Iran war until it gets Hormuz reopened — Axios
stories.description: As long as the Iranian blockade holds, and Gulf oil is restricted, Washington can not end its military operation even If it wanted to, the officials said

stories.title: Resource-hungry US tries to poach Iran's uranium — Lavrov
stories.description: The Russian foreign minister added that Washington wants to get practically all hydrocarbon resources

stories.title: Five countries refuse to join US coalition in Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: China and France pointed to the need for a diplomatic settlement of the dispute in the region

stories.title: Lack of response to strikes on Iran may prompt attacks on other countries — diplomat
stories.description: Ali Bahreini said that "Iran was attacked at the time when it peacefully engaged in negotiations, clearly confirming its commitment to peaceful and diplomatic resolution"

stories.title: EU has no money to mitigate energy shock caused by conflict in Iran — WSJ
stories.description: According to the newspaper, the public debt of some European countries, particularly the UK and France, is reaching 60-year highs

stories.title: Restoring traffic in Strait of Hormuz to take 2-3 years — former Iranian envoy to Russia
stories.description: According to earlier statements by a spokesman for the Iranian Armed Forces’ Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters, Iran would not allow any oil cargo related to the United States or its allies to pass through the Strait of Hormuz

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
